message:
Last Tuesday I was making grilled cheese for lunch — you know, that zone-out moment where you're scrolling your phone and suddenly *smell* something. My smoke alarm went off before I even saw the char on the pan.

I've honestly replaced that battery twice since we moved in, and I almost didn't bother the last time. But I did. And that little beep? It caught my attention fast enough that I just turned off the heat, cracked a window, and laughed at myself instead of dealing with actual flames.

Small win. Honestly made me realize how many of those "little" prep moments actually matter when it counts — and how easy it is to skip them because life gets busy.

Have you checked yours lately? (No judgment — I forget too.)
